what_it_does: A ledger of visual spacing and padding findings extracted from multiple pitch-deck and landing-page design substrates, cataloging observed margin, gutter, card padding, column split, and vertical rhythm patterns as prescriptive generator rules.
observations: Each finding follows a consistent schema: category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent flag, generator_rule, _src, and _depth.
Most categories are 'padding-alignment' with a few 'emergent-spacing' entries.
Sources include capital-raise, company-info, deck1-2026, pitch-deck, presentation-15p, product-vi-landing, product-vt, and recent-pitches.
Recurring patterns: equal outer margins, equal card padding on all sides, strict equal-width column grids, 50/50 or 45/55 splits, and an 8px-based modular vertical rhythm.
Many entries cite slide/page identifiers as evidence (e.g., p-02, lp1, architects/p-1).
Numerical values are often approximate pixel ranges or percentages of slide width, and they vary across substrates.
Some findings are flagged emergent=true and capture relational patterns such as card padding equaling gutter or asymmetric vertical breathing.
The file is truncated near the end; the last finding is incomplete.
standouts: Equal-padding computation: card internal padding and card gutters are set to the same token value.
Strict equal-width column grids (2, 3, or 4 columns) with computed widths, never mixed or eyeballed.
Asymmetric vertical breathing: more space above body content than below, elevating the title and grounding the footer.
The V-logo mark functions as a consistent bottom-right margin anchor across deck variants.
8px modular grid used as the rhythm engine for vertical spacing.
conventions: Findings use a stable schema with descriptive fields and a prescriptive generator_rule.
Generator rules are imperative and frequently include prohibitions ('never', 'do not').
_depth describes the source compression level (1-page, 15-page, 30-page, long-form).
Measurements mix pixel estimates, percentage-of-width values, and qualitative terms like 'approximately' and 'estimated'.
concerns: The file is truncated; the final entry cuts off mid-sentence.
Margin and padding ranges differ across sources without explicit scaling or token resolution, risking conflict if fused directly.
Some measurements are visually estimated rather than exact.
There are no cross-reference IDs or versioning to reconcile duplicate concepts across _src decks.
notes: A spacing-rule audit trail that converts observed padding, margins, gutters, and vertical rhythm from many deck variants into prescriptive generator rules.
questions: What is the canonical canvas resolution and base spacing token that these pixel ranges map to?
How should conflicting margin ranges from different substrates be reconciled (e.g., 40-50px vs 52-60px vs 5-6%)?
Is the V-logo mark size and inset fixed, or does it scale with deck format?
What process determines whether a finding is marked emergent?
Does the truncated final entry continue in another file?
purpose_vs_actual: The file does what it appears intended to do: catalog observed spacing patterns and turn them into generator rules. It is incomplete due to truncation.
apparent_themes: Computed, equal spacing as a marker of quality and intentional design.
Rhythmic vertical spacing built on modular scales, often 8px.
Consistency across slides and decks through anchored margins and repeated logo placement.
Whitespace used as a structural divider instead of visible rules.
Compression-invariant language: the same spacing principles expressed at 1-page, 15-page, and 30-page depths.
intention_signals: To extract and codify the implicit spacing grammar of a design system so future decks can be generated with the same measured, breathable, computed layout rhythm.
novelty: The emergent-spacing entries capture second-order relationships (e.g., padding equals gutter, asymmetric vertical breathing) that treat spacing as a relational system rather than isolated values.
contribution: A structured set of spacing heuristics and prescriptive generator rules drawn from multiple deck variants, useful as input to a fusion system that must resolve margin, gutter, padding tokens, and vertical rhythm.
summary_for_embedding: spacing.findings.json is a structured array of visual spacing observations from pitch decks and landing pages, mapping margins, gutters, card padding, column splits, and vertical rhythm into generator rules with source and depth metadata.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the implicit spacing grammar of a design system so it can be reproduced consistently across deck compressions and formats.
